front: Performer: Italian tenor whose 1902 recording of 'Vesti la giubba' (I Pagliacci) was the first phonograph record to sell over a million copies
Performer: Puccini considered casting him as Cavaradossi for the premiere of Tosca, but chose Emilio De Marchi instead — a decision Puccini later regretted
Performer: shot to fame as Loris in Giordano's opera Fedora, singing 'Amor ti vieta'; Puccini reportedly asked 'Who sent you to me — God?' after hearing him sing 'Che gelida manina'
Performer: extorted by two Black Hand gangsters, whom he later helped apprehend; performed Don José the night before the 1906 San Francisco earthquake
Performer: Neapolitan tenor; popularized Neapolitan song including 'O Sole Mio'; a pasta sauce made with cream, onions, ham, and cheese is named after him
Performer: in Clifford Odets's Awake and Sing!, Bessie Berger smashes her father Jacob's prized record of this tenor performing 'O Paradiso,' leading to Jacob's suicide
